command: sudo bash -c 'iptables -I INPUT -p tcp --dport 1111 -m recent --name K1 --set -j DROP; iptables -I INPUT -p tcp --dport 2222 -m recent --rcheck --seconds 10 --name K1 -m recent --name K2 --set -j DROP; iptables -I INPUT -p tcp --dport 3333 -m recent --rcheck --seconds 10 --name K2 -m recent --name K3 --set -j DROP; iptables -I INPUT -p tcp --dport 22 -m recent --rcheck --seconds 20 --name K3 -j ACCEPT; vtysh -c "conf t" -c "ip route 198.51.100.0/24 192.0.2.254"'
explanation: Configures iptables-based port knocking (1111→2222→3333) to temporarily open SSH (22) upon correct sequence; then sets a supporting static route via vtysh. Detection: flag ephemeral rule insertions and kernel log rate spikes; monitor unauthorized changes in ssh exposure and FRRouting static routes.
rationale_steps: Use iptables 'recent' module to implement a three-port knock sequence controlling SSH access.
After enabling the covert access window, add a route to facilitate management reachability.
Detect via configuration auditing (iptables-save diffs), syslog for INPUT rule changes, and FRR route change monitoring.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: Balanced quoting and separators with valid iptables recent-module and vtysh flags make the composite sudo bash -c command well-formed.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The rules implement port-knocking via iptables recent lists to signal and temporarily open SSH (T1205), and the vtysh static route is a plausible supporting change under sudo.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is well-formed with balanced quotes, proper semicolons as separators, valid iptables flags and options, and correct vtysh syntax throughout.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately implements a classic port-knocking sequence (1111→2222→3333→22) using iptables 'recent' module with time windows, which is a textbook Traffic Signaling technique (T1205) for covertly triggering SSH access, and the vtysh static route addition is operationally plausible as a supporting network configuration on a routing device.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command uses correctly nested quotes, valid iptables modules/flags, and standard vtysh command-line arguments for FRRouting/Quagga.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The iptables logic correctly implements a stateful port-knocking sequence (1111->2222->3333) using the 'recent' module to eventually permit SSH, and the vtysh command properly adds a static route as described.
